name: Embalse de Alange — circular by the Sierra de Peñas Blancas foothills
name_original: Embalse de Alange — circular por la falda de Sierra de Peñas Blancas
region_intro: Touring cycling around Villagonzalo Pedernales offers routes across an elevated landscape with plains reaching up to 900 meters, situated on the Páramos de Arlanzón. This terrain features extensive cereal fields and natural vegetation, providing varied scenery for cyclists. The municipality is also located south of the Arlanzón River valley, adding diverse natural features to the cycling experience. These conditions create a suitable environment for exploring the region by bike. Best touring cycling routes around Villagonzalo The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Alange — circular by the Sierra de Peñas Blancas foothills, a 27.1 miles (43.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the foothills. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alange Reservoir – Roman Monuments of Mérida loop from Alange, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.2 km) path. This route combines reservoir views with historical Roman sites. Local touring cyclists also love the Roman Monuments of Mérida – Acueducto de los Milagros loop from Don Álvaro, a 28.2 miles (45.4 km) trail leading through historical landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Villagonzalo is defined by elevated plains, extensive cereal fields, and the Arlanzón River valley. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Villagonzalo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villagonzalo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Villagonzalo Pedernales?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around Villagonzalo Pedernales, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Villagonzalo Pedernales?
A: The terrain around Villagonzalo Pedernales features an elevated landscape with plains reaching up to 900 meters in altitude, situated on the Páramos de Arlanzón. You'll encounter extensive cereal fields, natural vegetation, and varied scenery as the municipality is also located south of the Arlanzón River valley. Routes can range from moderate to difficult, often including significant elevation gain.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The touring cycling routes around Villagonzalo Pedernales primarily cater to moderate and difficult ability levels. There are 11 moderate routes and 4 difficult routes, ensuring options for cyclists looking for a challenge or a steady ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Villagonzalo Pedernales?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in the area are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022438708/embalse-de-alange-circular-by-the-sierra-de-penas-blancas-foothills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alange — circular by the Sierra de Peñas Blancas foothills&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir – Roman Monuments of Mérida loop from Alange&lt;/a&gt;, which combines reservoir views with historical sites.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6046286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Monuments of Mérida – Acueducto de los Milagros loop from Don Álvaro&lt;/a&gt;, will lead you through historical landscapes, showcasing the rich heritage of the area.

Q: Are there any routes that combine cycling with views of lakes or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. Several routes offer stunning views of local water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir – Roman Monuments of Mérida loop from Alange&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, providing scenic reservoir vistas. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Alange Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; along various paths.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Villagonzalo Pedernales?
A: The elevated plains and varied landscapes of Villagonzalo Pedernales make it suitable for touring cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, especially in the open cereal fields, while winters can be chilly. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any longer touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders looking for longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31497737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Don Álvaro&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 46.3 km with significant elevation gain, offering a challenging and rewarding experience. The region is also part of the Camino del Cid, a long-distance route that passes through the province of Burgos.

Q: What can I expect regarding elevation gain on these routes?
A: Given the elevated landscape of the Páramos de Arlanzón, you can expect varying but often significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022438708/embalse-de-alange-circular-by-the-sierra-de-penas-blancas-foothills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alange — circular by the Sierra de Peñas Blancas foothills&lt;/a&gt; involves over 460 meters of ascent, making it a challenging ride.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a moderate touring cycling experience?
A: Yes, the majority of routes in the area are classified as moderate. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38226487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Railroad Crossing – Farm Track loop from Calamonte&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a manageable distance and elevation for a pleasant touring cycling experience.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while cycling?
A: The natural landscape around Villagonzalo Pedernales is characterized by typical steppe fauna. While cycling through the cereal fields and natural vegetation, you might spot animals such as rabbits, foxes, and partridges.

Q: Is there public transport access to the cycling routes around Villagonzalo Pedernales?
A: Villagonzalo Pedernales is located just 8 kilometers from the city of Burgos, which is well-connected by public transport. While specific public transport links directly to trailheads might be limited, accessing the general area via Burgos and then cycling to the starting points is a viable option.
tips: Arriving in the town of Alange and with the Alange Reservoir at its feet, from a distance, you can see the center with the hill where the Culebra Castle resides. This elevated construction is visible above the rest of the territory.
Going towards the town of Palomas you will find a series of agricultural fields that abound around the road. Depending on the time of year, the fields are an intense green, forming a spectacle.

The reservoir, during its construction, flooded some houses located in the lower area of Alange as well as the hamlet of Los Pajares, belonging to the town of La Zarza. In order to remedy this problem, the affected people were rehoused in a new neighborhood located on the La Zarza road
